title: Chrome DevTools MCP Server
permitted_uses: Supports local browser inspection, interaction, and visual verification during Docusaurus site development.
Supports agentic acceptance testing of the docs site UI by enabling agents to navigate pages, take screenshots, and evaluate JavaScript in a live Chrome instance.
Supports local development workflow verification — agents can open the dev server, interact with it, and confirm the golden path renders correctly.
claims_supported: The chrome-devtools-mcp server provides 40+ tools across 8 categories: input automation, navigation, debugging, performance, network, memory, extensions, and emulation.
It connects to a live Chrome instance via MCP and is compatible with Claude Code and Codex sub-agents via project-scoped .mcp.json configuration.
Requires Node.js v20.19+ and current stable Chrome. Configured in this repo via .mcp.json at project scope.
A lightweight slim mode with 3 essential tools (screenshot, navigate, evaluate) is available for basic UI verification.
claims_not_supported: The MCP server replaces acceptance tests — it is a local visibility tool, not a substitute for pnpm test coverage.
It supports browsers other than Google Chrome/Chrome for Testing.
